zh.lifeSummary: 當Emeline Dauney於1330年出生在英國林可爾史爾英格爾斯比時，她的父親Sir Knight John Dawnay28歲，母親Sibly De TREVERBYN24歲。她於1356年與Sir Edward Courtenay在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1371年2月28日之前在英國戴文哈可姆，埋葬在英國可瓦爾維頓可聖 馬也斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Dauney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name from Darney or Darney-aux-Chênes in the Vosges.
English and Scottish: variant of Dorney , with Gloucestershire unrounding of o.
Americanized form of German Dany: from a short form of the personal name Daniel .

variants.name: Dorney
meanings: Irish (Cork and Tipperary): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Doirinne ‘descendant of Doireann’, a female personal name meaning ‘sullen’.
Scottish: probably a diminutive variant of Scottish Durno, a habitational name from the placename Derno (Fife) or, less probably, the placename Dornoch (Sutherland).

variants.name: Emily
meanings: From a medieval form of the Latin name Aemilia, the feminine version of the old Roman family name Aemilius (probably from aemulus ‘rival’). It was not common in the Middle Ages, but was revived in the 19th century and is extremely popular throughout the English-speaking world today. Its best-known 19th‐century bearer was probably the novelist and poet Emily Brontë ( 1818–48 ).

variants.name: Emma
meanings: Old French name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, originally a short form of compound names such as Ermintrude containing the word erm(en), irm(en) ‘entire’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, but its popularity in medieval England was greatly enhanced by the fact that it had been borne by the mother of Edward the Confessor, herself a Norman. In modern times, it was only in moderate use early in the 20th century but rose sharply in favour in the 1970s and has since remained perennially popular.

variants.name: Emmeline
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. In origin it seems to have been a derivative of   Emma  , but when it was revived in the 19th century there was some confusion with   Emily .  It was born by the suffragette  Emmeline Pankhurst  (  1858–1928 ), mother of  Christabel  and  Sylvia .

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Emeline Dauney was born in 1330, in Ingoldsby, Lincolnshire, England, her father, Sir Knight John Dawnay, was 28 and her mother, Sibly De TREVERBYN, was 24. She married Sir Edward Courtenay in 1356, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died before 28 February 1371, in Haccombe, Devon, England, and was buried in St Mary's Church, Sheviock, Cornwall, England.

fr.St Mary's Church, Sheviock, Cornwall, En: St Mary's Church, Sheviock, Cornouailles, Angleterre
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Emeline Dauney est née en 1330, à Ingoldsby, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Knight John Dawnay, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Sibly De TREVERBYN, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Edward Courtenay en 1356, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée avant 28 février 1371, à Haccombe, Devon, Angleterre, et a été enterrée à St Mary's Church, Sheviock, Cornouailles, Angleterre.

de.St Mary's Church, Sheviock, Cornwall, En: St Mary's Church, Sheviock, Cornwall, England
de.lifeSummary: Als Emeline Dauney 1330 in Ingoldsby, Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Knight John Dawnay 28 und ihre Mutter Sibly De TREVERBYN 24. Sie hat 1356 in England, United Kingdom Sir Edward Courtenay geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb vor 28. Februar 1371 in Haccombe, Devon, England und wurde in St Mary's Church, Sheviock, Cornwall, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Emeline Dauney è nata il nel 1330 nel Ingoldsby, Lincolnshire, England, sua padre, Sir Knight John Dawnay, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Sibly De TREVERBYN, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato Sir Edward Courtenay nel 1356, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì prima del 28 febbraio 1371, nel Haccombe, Devon, England, e fu sepolta a St Mary's Church, Sheviock, Cornwall, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Emeline Dauneyが1330年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアインゴールドスビーで生まれた時，父Sir Knight John Dawnayは28歳，母Sibly De TREVERBYNは24歳であった。1356年にイギリスイギリスでSir Edward Courtenayと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1371年2月28日以前にイギリスデボンハッコインベで死去し，イギリスコルンワルシェヴィオックスト マリ'ス チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.St Mary's Church, Sheviock, Cornwall, En: 영국 코른월 셰비오크 스트 마이'스 처르치
ko.lifeSummary: Emeline Dauney(이)가 1330년 영국 링코실르 잉드스비에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Knight John Dawnay(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sibly De TREVERBYN(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1356년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Sir Edward Courtenay(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1371년 2월 28일 이전 영국 데폰 하치브에서 사망했으며 영국 코른월 셰비오크 스트 마이'스 처르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Emeline Dauney nasceu em 1330, em Ingoldsby, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Knight John Dawnay, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Sibly De TREVERBYN, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Edward Courtenay em 1356, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ela faleceu antes 28 de fevereiro de 1371, em Haccombe, Devon, Inglaterra, e foi sepultada em St Mary's Church, Sheviock, Cornwall, Inglaterra.

ru.St Mary's Church, Sheviock, Cornwall, En: ст мэри'с чурч, шевиокк, корнвалл, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Emeline Dauney родилась в 1330 г. в местности инголдсби, линколншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Knight John Dawnay было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Sibly De TREVERBYN, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Edward Courtenay в 1356 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла перед 28 февраль 1371 г. в местности хаккомб, девон, Англия и была похоронена в местности ст мэри'с чурч, шевиокк, корнвалл, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Emeline Dauney nació en 1330, en Ingoldsby, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Knight John Dawnay, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Sibly De TREVERBYN, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Sir Edward Courtenay en 1356, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió antes de 28 de febrero de 1371, en Haccombe, Devon, Inglaterra, y fue sepultada en St Mary's Church, Sheviock, Cornwall, Inglaterra.
